title: Boat Rental in Galapagos: How to Choose the Right Vessel in 2026
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in the Galapagos Islands requires matching vessel type to your itinerary, budget, and group size. From Puerto Ayora on Santa Cruz, expect to pay from $150 for a half-day shared tour to over $2,000 for a private crewed yacht. BoatHire24 helps you compare verified listings across the archipelago.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Galapagos?

Prices range from $100 per person for a shared tour to $3,000 per day for a private catamaran. The final cost depends on boat size, duration, crew, and fuel. A half-day private speedboat for up to six guests typically costs $400, $800, while a full-day crewed yacht for twelve guests may be $1,500, $3,000. Always confirm whether park fees, meals, and snorkelling gear are included. For the latest prices and availability, you can rent a boat directly through BoatHire24.

Which boat should I choose for my Galapagos trip?

Your choice depends on your budget, group size, and desired itinerary. For day trips between islands, a fast motorboat or speedboat is ideal. For overnight expeditions, a catamaran or yacht offers comfort and range.

  1. Speedboat, best for half-day trips to nearby islets; up to 8 guests; from $400 half-day. See this speedboat as an example.
  2. Catamaran, stable, spacious, ideal for full-day voyages; up to 16 guests; from $1,200 full-day.
  3. Motor Yacht, comfortable for multi-day charters; up to 10 guests; from $2,000 per day.
  4. Sailing Yacht, for a classic experience; up to 6 guests; from $800 per day.
  5. RIB, nimble and fun for small groups; up to 6 guests; from $300 half-day.

Where to set off in Galapagos

Most charters depart from the main islands of Santa Cruz, San Cristóbal, and Isabela. The main departure points are Puerto Ayora on Santa Cruz, Puerto Baquerizo Moreno on San Cristóbal, and Puerto Villamil on Isabela. Other common anchorages include Academy Bay, Wreck Bay, and the calm waters of Tortuga Bay. For northern itineraries, boats often leave from the port at Baltra, the main airport island.

Cost breakdown by trip type

  • Shared tour (catamaran, 10, 16 guests): $100, $200 per person, half-day
  • Private speedboat (up to 6 guests): $400, $800, half-day
  • Private catamaran (up to 16 guests): $1,200, $2,000, full-day
  • Crewed yacht (up to 10 guests, with meals): $2,000, $3,000, full-day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Galapagos?

No licence is required to charter a crewed boat, but bareboat rentals typically demand an international certificate. All vessels on BoatHire24 are offered with a qualified skipper unless you specifically request bareboat. The operator will confirm exact requirements before booking. Local regulations require a licensed naturalist guide on most trips.

Tip: book a crewed boat for stress-free navigation; the skipper knows local currents and wildlife hotspots like the Bolívar Channel.

At a glance

  • Price band: $100, $3,000 USD per day
  • Peak months: June to November (garúa season, moderate seas)
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 7 days
  • Capacity: 4, 16 guests
  • Currency: US dollar (USD)

Questions about boat rental in Galapagos

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Most operators require a skipper, but a few offer bareboat hire with proof of a valid boating licence and local knowledge.

What is the best season for a private charter?

The dry season from June to November offers cooler waters and better visibility for diving, while the wet season from December to May has warmer seas and calmer winds.

How far in advance should I book?

During peak season (June, November), book at least three months ahead; for last-minute trips, check cancelled slots on BoatHire24.

Are meals included in the charter price?

Many full-day and multi-day charters include lunch and snacks; always confirm the inclusions in the listing.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Galapagos or list your own. For more than 30 years, the archipelago has drawn visitors to its unique marine life, and chartering a private vessel remains the best way to explore its remote coves and snorkelling sites. If you own a boat, you can browse the listings to see how to become a host and start earning income from your vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Galapagos and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
